questions.q: On May 11, 2026, the European Union approved new sanctions against violent Israeli settlers in the West Bank after which country's former government dropped its veto?
A study highlighted in May 2026 reported a 74% drop in toxic PFAS "forever chemicals" over a 55-year period in the eggs of which Canadian seabird?
According to a May 2026 economic intelligence report, North Korea earned up to $14 billion—nearly half of its GDP—over three years by providing military support to which nation?
In May 2026, 12 Radboud hospital employees in the Netherlands were placed in quarantine following the incorrect handling of a patient infected with which virus?
In a major corruption probe announced on May 11, 2026, Andriy Yermak, the former second-in-command to the President of which country, was charged with money laundering?
